data.title: Precore Gold closes first tranche of private placement, raising C$1,000,029.60 with 5,555,720 units sold at C$0.18 per unit.
data.description: Precore Gold Corp. (CSE: PRCG) based in Vancouver, British Columbia, reported the completion of the initial tranche of its non-brokered private placement, as of September 18, 2025. The private placement, initially announced on August 21, 2025, yielded total gross proceeds of C$1,000,029.60. This phase involved the sale of 5,555,720 units at a price of C$0.18 per unit, each unit comprising one common share and one-half of a common share purchase warrant. The warrant entitles the holder to acquire one additional common share for C$0.25 per warrant share within a thirty-six-month period.

data.title: Historic soccer showdown: Argentina vs Puerto Rico at Soldier Field on October 13, 2025, capacity 61,500. Part of AFA U.S. Friendly Tour.
data.description: Chicago, (BUSINESS WIRE) - VMG Sports & Entertainment, in partnership with the Argentine Football Association (AFA) and the Puerto Rican Football Federation (FPF), is excited to announce a unique international friendly match: Argentina vs Puerto Rico. The match is set to take place on Monday, October 13, 2025, at Soldier Field in Chicago at 7 PM CT. Soldier Field has a capacity of 61,500. This event is a part of the AFA U.S. Friendly Tour leading up to the 2026 FIFA World Cup, which kicks off with Argentina facing off against Venezuela in Miami.

data.title: TransAlta (TSX: TA) to host Investor Day in Toronto on Nov 18, 2025 from 9:00 am - 11:30 am (ET).
data.description: An upcoming Investor Day by TransAlta Corporation, a leading company in the sector (TSX: TA) (NYSE: TAC), will take place in Toronto on November 18, 2025. The event is scheduled to start at 9:00 a.m. (ET) (7:00 a.m. MT) and is anticipated to conclude by about 11:30 a.m. (ET) (9:30 a.m. MT).

data.title: Gevo partners with Biorecro for carbon credit deal, boosting revenue stream and expanding business with an in-operation project.
data.description: Gevo's carbon division is growing, providing a dependable revenue stream for the company while enhancing Biorecro's BECCS project lineup with a new project in operation. This expansion signifies a significant increase in revenue sources for Gevo and highlights the success of Biorecro's BECCS initiatives.

data.title: Scholastic reports fiscal Q1 2026 results, $225.6M revenue, $92.2M operating loss, and $55.7M adjusted EBITDA; focus on growing strategic initiatives.
data.description: Scholastic Corporation reported its financial results for the fiscal first quarter ended August 31, 2025. The company experienced a seasonal operating loss of $92.2 million, which was expected due to schools not being in session during the summer. Despite this, Scholastic made progress in strategic initiatives, particularly in the Children's Book Group segment with encouraging fall book fair bookings. Adjusted EBITDA improved by 8% to a loss of $55.7 million. Revenues decreased by 5% to $225.6 million, with the Education Solutions segment experiencing a 28% decline due to funding uncertainties. The Children's Book Publishing and Distribution segment saw a 4% increase in revenues to $109.4 million. The Entertainment segment revenues decreased by 18% to $13.6 million, and International revenues increased by 4% to $59.4 million. The company is affirming its fiscal 2026 guidance and remains focused on strengthening its balance sheet for long-term growth and impact. Scholastic retained Newmark Group for potential sale-leaseback transactions to monetize its real estate assets. The company had a net cash use of $100.2 million and net debt of $242.8 million in the first quarter. They distributed $5.2 million in dividends and plan to continue purchasing shares for the foreseeable future. Investors can access additional financial information through non-GAAP calculations and presentations. CEO Peter Warwick and CFO Haji Glover will discuss the results in a conference call on September 18, 2025.

data.title: Lawmakers question SEC on Tron IPO, advocate for investigation of Justin Sun. SEC paused case in Feb, Tron went public on Nasdaq.
data.description: In February, the financial regulator requested a judge to pause its enforcement action against the Tron founder. Subsequently, the company made its debut on Nasdaq.

data.title: Digital Empathy launches Spotlight for vet practices after 2 years of development, 8 months beta. New tech debut at VHMA conference.
data.description: In San Diego, Digital Empathy recently announced the official launch of its inaugural technology product, Spotlight, following a two-year development period and an eight-month beta phase. The unveiling occurred at the Veterinary Hospital Managers Association (VHMA) conference in New Orleans. This release marks a significant milestone for the company. Concurrently, Digital Empathy also introduced a revamped website that showcases its refined focus on strategic growth and marketing services.
